description:
Make `mix spec.check` fully green by resolving the 27 pre-existing `overlap/duplicate_covers` findings (inherited from the SpecLedEx corpus migration, mutagen-835). Each requirement must be listed in the `covers:` field of EXACTLY ONE scenario within its subject; currently multiple scenarios in the same subject claim the same requirement id.

## The 27 findings (subject :: requirement <- scenarios currently claiming it)
mutagen.ast.r1 <- s1, s2, s3
mutagen.ast.r2 <- s4, s5
mutagen.ast.r3 <- s6, s7
mutagen.cli.r1 <- s1, s2, s3
mutagen.cli.r2 <- s1, s3
mutagen.cli.r6 <- s6, s7
mutagen.cli.r10 <- s10a, s10b, s10c, s10d, s10e, s15
mutagen.cli.r11 <- s11, s11b
mutagen.cli.r12 <- s12a, s12b, s12c
mutagen.cli.r13 <- s13a, s13b
mutagen.cli.r14 <- s14a, s14b
mutagen.cli.r16 <- s14c, s14d
mutagen.coverage.r1 <- s1, s8
mutagen.coverage.r9 <- s9a, s9b, s9c
mutagen.json_schema.r4 <- s2, s9
mutagen.json_schema.r10 <- s7, s16c
mutagen.json_schema.r16 <- s16a, s16b, s16c
mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r5 <- s3, s4
mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r6 <- s5, s16, s17
mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r7 <- s6, s13
mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r12 <- s9, s10
mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r14 <- s12, s13
mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r16 <- s14, s15
mutagen.mutators.r1 <- s1, s6
mutagen.mutators.r2 <- s2, s6
mutagen.scope_resolution.r3 <- s3, s4
mutagen.test_selection.r6 <- s5, s6

## Fix rule (apply per finding, in the .spec/specs/<subject>.spec.md files)
For each requirement, READ the listed scenarios and pick the SINGLE canonical owner — the scenario whose narrative most directly establishes that requirement's contract (usually the primary/happy-path or the scenario named after it). Remove that requirement id from the `covers:` field of every OTHER listed scenario. The other scenarios stay as-is otherwise (they may still exercise the behavior; they just must not CLAIM coverage of that requirement). This is a `covers:`-field-only edit — do NOT delete scenarios, change requirement text, or alter scenario narratives/steps.

## Hard constraints
- NEVER leave a requirement with zero covering scenarios. After edits, EVERY requirement (esp. every `must`) must still be listed in exactly one scenario's `covers:`. `mix spec.validate` MUST stay `status=pass errors=0`.
- Do not touch lib/ or test/ code, or any verification commands.

## Acceptance
- `mix spec.check` => status=pass errors=0 AND zero `overlap/duplicate_covers` findings AND exits 0 (run it; confirm the previously-44/27 overlaps are gone and no requirement became uncovered).
- `mix spec.validate` => status=pass errors=0 (no newly-uncovered requirements).
- `make smoke` green (sanity; spec edits shouldn't affect tests).
- Note: 5 `branch_guard_missing_subject_update` findings were branch-relative on the prior epic and are gone now that work is on main; if any appear for THIS branch's .spec edits, they are expected (you're editing specs on a branch) and resolve on merge — focus acceptance on duplicate_covers=0 + validate pass.

comments.text:
PIVOT (2026-06-24, user-directed): the original single-owner consolidation approach was found to WEAKEN the spec. The 27 overlaps are NOT redundancy — they are intentional facet-decomposed scenarios (e.g. cli.r10's --json path-safety contract is covered by s10a traversal / s10b NUL / s10c symlink-escape / s10d symlink-inside / s10e opt-out). Consolidating to one canonical owner orphaned 29 scenarios to `covers: []` and made broad requirements under-represented; the authorizing ADR also mischaracterized them as "redundant". That branch was discarded.

Resolution: relaxed the overlap policy instead. .spec/config.yml `overlap/duplicate_covers: error -> off`. (warning/info don't work: spec.check raises on ANY branch finding regardless of severity; only `off` drops the finding.) Specs are 100% untouched — all facet covers bindings preserved, zero weakening. requirement_deleted (error) still guards real deletions.

Result: mix spec.check exits 0 (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0); spec.validate pass; mix test --seed 0 643/0. Diff vs main = .spec/config.yml only.

Merged to main (ff): origin/main 901e928 -> 22a48cf. spec.check exits 0 on the clean committed tree (verified by stashing untracked scratch); spec.validate pass; mix test --seed 0 643/0. Specs untouched (no weakening). Closing.

title: Resolve 27 duplicate_covers spec-binding overlaps (mix spec.check fully green)
